<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are reviewing PR #5 on /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve (branch feat/target-color-input vs main). You have one assigned task in the team task list — read it first via TaskList, then claim it via TaskUpdate (owner: "feature-reviewer", status: in_progress).

Scope: Target color feature only. Focus on the new TargetSwatch (~326 lines), TargetFormats, lib/colors.ts (new, 90 lines), paletteStore additions (32 lines), and types/palette.ts changes.

What to look for (high-confidence findings only — don't pad):
- Color parsing/formatting correctness (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch — whatever formats are supported). Use chroma.js as the source of truth.
- Format-matched copy feedback: does it actually match the format the user is viewing?
- Edge cases: invalid input handling, alpha channel, out-of-gamut colors, empty/whitespace input.
- Accessibility: keyboard interaction, ARIA labels, focus management on the input + format selector.
- State management: any redundant state, stale closures, missed memoization that would cause perceptible jank.
- Token compliance: no hardcoded HSL values, no opacity-on-text (`white/60`, `text-white/50` etc.) — see /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/.claude/CLAUDE.md.

Use git diff origin/main...HEAD -- <paths> to see exactly what changed. Use rg/fd, not grep/find.

Report back via SendMessage to "team-lead" with a concise findings list grouped by severity (Critical / High / Medium / Low / Nit). Each finding: file:line, what's wrong, why it matters, suggested fix. If everything looks good in a category, say so in one line. Then mark your task completed via TaskUpdate.
</teammate-message>

```text
diff --git a/app/src/lib/colors.ts b/app/src/lib/colors.ts
index 9e03f04..3527230 100644
--- a/app/src/lib/colors.ts
+++ b/app/src/lib/colors.ts
@@ -7,6 +7,77 @@ export function getContrastColor(bgColor: string): string {
   return whiteContrast < WCAG_AA_CONTRAST ? "#1d1d1d" : "#ffffff";
 }
 
+export type ColorFormat = "hex" | "hsl" | "rgb" | "oklch" | "named";
+
+/**
+ * Parse a color string and extract hue (0-360), saturation (0-1), and input format.
+ * Supports hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), and named CSS colors.
+ */
+export function parseTargetColor(
+  input: string,
+): { hue: number; saturation: number; format: ColorFormat } | null {
+  const trimmed = input.trim();
+  if (!trimmed) return null;
+
+  try {
+    // oklch() - chroma.js can't parse this natively
+    const oklchMatch = trimmed.match(
+      /oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i,
+    );
+    if (oklchMatch) {
+      let l = parseFloat(oklchMatch[1]);
+      if (oklchMatch[1].endsWith("%")) l /= 100;
+      const c = parseFloat(oklchMatch[2]);
+      const h = parseFloat(oklchMatch[3]);
+      const [hue, sat] = chroma.oklch(l, c, h).hsl();
+      return {
+        hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
+        saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
+        format: "oklch",
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Detect format before parsing
+    const lower = trimmed.toLowerCase();
+    let format: ColorFormat = "named";
+    if (lower.startsWith("#")) format = "hex";
+    else if (lower.startsWith("hsl")) format = "hsl";
+    else if (lower.startsWith("rgb")) format = "rgb";
+
+    const [hue, sat] = chroma(trimmed).hsl();
+    return {
+      hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
+      saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
+      format,
+    };
+  } catch {
+    return null;
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Format a color at hue/sat/L=50 in the given format.
+ */
+export function formatTargetColor(
+  hue: number,
+  saturation: number,
+  format: ColorFormat,
+): string {
+  const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5);
+  switch (format) {
+    case "hsl":
+      return `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;
+    case "rgb": {
+      const [r, g, b] = color.rgb();
+      return `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;
+    }
+    case "oklch":
+      return toOklchString(color.hex());
+    default:
+      return color.hex();
+  }
+}
+
 export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
   const [l, c, h] = chroma(hex).oklch();
   const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
@@ -14,3 +85,22 @@ export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
   const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
   return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
 }
+
+export interface AllFormats {
+  hex: string;
+  rgb: string;
+  hsl: string;
+  oklch: string;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Return all four string representations of a color at hue/sat/L=50.
+ */
+export function formatAllFormats(hue: number, saturation: number): AllFormats {
+  return {
+    hex: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hex"),
+    rgb: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "rgb"),
+    hsl: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hsl"),
+    oklch: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "oklch"),
+  };
+}

@@ -25,10 +37,30 @@ export const usePaletteStore = create<PaletteState>((set) => ({

   setSatMode: (satMode) => set({ satMode }),
   setLRange: (lMin, lMax) => set({ lMin, lMax }),

+  setTarget: (hue, saturation, input, format) =>
+    set({

+    }),
+  clearTarget: () =>

+      targetInput: null,
+      targetFormat: null,

 }));
```

 `./app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx`

```text
1	import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
2	import chroma from "chroma-js";
3	import * as Popover from "@radix-ui/react-popover";
4	import { X } from "@phosphor-icons/react";
5	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
6	import { parseTargetColor, formatTargetColor } from "../../lib/colors";
7	import type { ColorFormat } from "../../lib/colors";
8	
9	const PICKER_SIZE = 200;
10	
11	function ColorInput({
12	  currentHex,
13	  targetInput,
14	  targetFormat,
15	  hue,
16	  saturation,
17	  onApply,
18	}: {
19	  currentHex: string;
20	  targetInput: string | null;
21	  targetFormat: ColorFormat | null;
22	  hue: number;
23	  saturation: number;
24	  onApply: (
25	    hue: number,
26	    saturation: number,
27	    input: string,
28	    format: ColorFormat,
29	  ) => void;
30	}) {
31	  const [value, setValue] = useState("");
32	  const [isInvalid, setIsInvalid] = useState(false);
33	
34	  const targetFormatted = targetFormat
35	    ? formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, targetFormat)
36	    : currentHex;
37	
38	  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
39	    if (e.key === "Enter") {
40	      const result = parseTargetColor(value);
41	      if (result) {
42	        onApply(result.hue, result.saturation, value.trim(), result.format);
43	        setValue("");
44	        setIsInvalid(false);
45	      } else {
46	        setIsInvalid(true);
47	      }
48	    } else if (e.key === "Escape") {
49	      setValue("");
50	      setIsInvalid(false);
51	      (e.target as HTMLInputElement).blur();
52	    }
53	  };
54	
55	  return (
56	    <div className="mt-2">
57	      <div className="mb-1.5">
58	        {targetInput ? (
59	          <div className="flex flex-col gap-0.5">
60	            <div className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5 font-mono text-xs">
61	              <span className="text-text-secondary">{targetInput}</span>
62	              <span className="text-text-faint">→</span>
63	              <span className="text-text-primary">{targetFormatted}</span>
64	            </div>
65	            <span className="text-4xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
66	              hue + saturation at L50
67	            </span>
68	          </div>
69	        ) : (
70	          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
71	            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
72	              Target
73	            </span>
74	            <span className="text-text-secondary font-mono text-xs">
75	              {currentHex}
76	            </span>
77	          </div>
78	        )}
79	      </div>
80	      <input
81	        type="text"
82	        value={value}
83	        onChange={(e) => {
84	          setValue(e.target.value);
85	          setIsInvalid(false);
86	        }}
87	        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
88	        placeholder="#hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch()"
89	        className={`bg-surface-base text-text-primary placeholder:text-text-faint w-full border px-2 py-1.5 font-mono text-xs transition-colors outline-none ${isInvalid ? "border-red-500" : "border-border-default focus:border-text-faint"}`}
90	      />
91	    </div>
92	  );
93	}
94	
95	export function TargetSwatch() {
96	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
97	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
98	  const setHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setHue);
99	  const setSaturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSaturation);
100	  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
101	  const targetFormat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetFormat);
102	  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
103	  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
104	  const setTarget = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setTarget);
105	  const clearTarget = usePaletteStore((s) => s.clearTarget);
106	
107	  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
108	  const draggingRef = useRef(false);
109	
110	  const targetActive = targetInput !== null;
111	  const targetDrifted =
112	    targetActive && (hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat);
113	
114	  const color = `hsl(${hue}, ${saturation * 100}%, 50%)`;
115	  const currentHex = useMemo(
116	    () => chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5).hex(),
117	    [hue, saturation],
118	  );
119	  const ringColor = useMemo(
120	    () =>
121	      targetActive && !targetDrifted && targetHue !== null
122	        ? `hsl(${targetHue}, 80%, 55%)`
123	        : null,
124	    [targetActive, targetDrifted, targetHue],
125	  );
126	
127	  const drawPicker = useCallback(
128	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => {
129	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
130	      if (!ctx) return;
131	      const w = PICKER_SIZE;
132	      const h = PICKER_SIZE;
133	
134	      // Draw hue (x) vs saturation (y) at L=50%
135	      const imageData = ctx.createImageData(w, h);
136	      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
137	        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
138	          const h360 = (x / (w - 1)) * 360;
139	          const s = 1 - y / (h - 1);
140	          // HSL to RGB conversion
141	          const c = (1 - Math.abs(2 * 0.5 - 1)) * s;
142	          const hp = h360 / 60;
143	          const x2 = c * (1 - Math.abs((hp % 2) - 1));
144	          let r1 = 0,
145	            g1 = 0,
146	            b1 = 0;
147	          if (hp < 1) {
148	            r1 = c;
149	            g1 = x2;
150	          } else if (hp < 2) {
151	            r1 = x2;
152	            g1 = c;
153	          } else if (hp < 3) {
154	            g1 = c;
155	            b1 = x2;
156	          } else if (hp < 4) {
157	            g1 = x2;
158	            b1 = c;
159	          } else if (hp < 5) {
160	            r1 = x2;
161	            b1 = c;
162	          } else {
163	            r1 = c;
164	            b1 = x2;
165	          }
166	          const m = 0.5 - c / 2;
167	          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;
168	          imageData.data[i] = Math.round((r1 + m) * 255);
169	          imageData.data[i + 1] = Math.round((g1 + m) * 255);
170	          imageData.data[i + 2] = Math.round((b1 + m) * 255);
171	          imageData.data[i + 3] = 255;
172	        }
173	      }
174	      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
175	
176	      // Draw crosshair at current hue/sat position
177	      const cx = (hue / 360) * (w - 1);
178	      const cy = (1 - saturation) * (h - 1);
179	      ctx.strokeStyle = "white";
180	      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
181	      ctx.beginPath();
182	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 5, 0, Math.PI * 2);
183	      ctx.stroke();
184	      ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)";
185	      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
186	      ctx.beginPath();
187	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 6, 0, Math.PI * 2);
188	      ctx.stroke();
189	    },
190	    [hue, saturation],
191	  );
192	
193	  const handleCanvasRef = useCallback(
194	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null) => {
195	      canvasRef.current = canvas;
196	      if (canvas) drawPicker(canvas);
197	    },
198	    [drawPicker],
199	  );
200	
201	  const pickFromCoords = useCallback(
202	    (clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
203	      const canvas = canvasRef.current;
204	      if (!canvas) return;
205	      const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
206	      if (
207	        clientX < rect.left ||
208	        clientX > rect.right ||
209	        clientY < rect.top ||
210	        clientY > rect.bottom
211	      )
212	        return;
213	      const x = (clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
214	      const y = (clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;
215	      clearTarget();
216	      setHue(Math.round(x * 360));
217	      setSaturation(parseFloat((1 - y).toFixed(2)));
218	    },
219	    [setHue, setSaturation, clearTarget],
220	  );
221	
222	  const handleMouseDown = useCallback(
223	    (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>) => {
224	      e.preventDefault();
225	      draggingRef.current = true;
226	      pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
227	    },
228	    [pickFromCoords],
229	  );
230	
231	  useEffect(() => {
232	    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
233	      if (draggingRef.current) pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
234	    };
235	    const handleMouseUp = () => {
236	      draggingRef.current = false;
237	    };
238	    document.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
239	    document.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
240	    return () => {
241	      document.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
242	      document.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
243	    };
244	  }, [pickFromCoords]);
245	
246	  return (
247	    <Popover.Root>
248	      <div className="group relative shrink-0 self-end">
249	        <Popover.Trigger asChild>
250	          <button
251	            type="button"
252	            className="flex cursor-pointer flex-col items-center gap-1"
253	            aria-label={
254	              targetActive
255	                ? `Target color: ${targetInput}${targetDrifted ? " (drifted)" : ""}`
256	                : `Current color: ${currentHex}`
257	            }
258	          >
259	            <div
260	              className="h-7 w-7 rounded-full transition-transform group-hover:scale-110"
261	              style={{
262	                backgroundColor: color,
263	                border: ringColor
264	                  ? `2px solid ${ringColor}`
265	                  : "2px solid var(--color-surface-base)",
266	                boxShadow: ringColor
267	                  ? `0 0 0 1px ${ringColor}`
268	                  : targetDrifted
269	                    ? "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)"
270	                    : "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.15)",
271	                opacity: targetDrifted ? 0.6 : 1,
272	              }}
273	            />
274	            <span
275	              className={`text-3xs font-mono transition-colors ${
276	                targetActive && !targetDrifted
277	                  ? "text-text-secondary"
278	                  : "text-text-faint"
279	              }`}
280	            >
281	              {currentHex}
282	            </span>
283	          </button>
284	        </Popover.Trigger>
285	        {targetActive && (
286	          <button
287	            type="button"
288	            onClick={clearTarget}
289	            aria-label="Clear target color"
290	            className="bg-surface-overlay border-border-elevated text-text-secondary hover:text-text-primary hover:bg-surface-hover absolute -top-1 -right-1 z-10 flex h-4 w-4 items-center justify-center rounded-full border opacity-0 transition-opacity group-hover:opacity-100"
291	          >
292	            <X size={10} weight="bold" />
293	          </button>
294	        )}
295	      </div>
296	      <Popover.Portal>
297	        <Popover.Content
298	          sideOffset={8}
299	          className="bg-surface-overlay border-border-elevated z-[60] rounded-none border p-3 shadow-xl"
300	        >
301	          <canvas
302	            ref={handleCanvasRef}
303	            width={PICKER_SIZE}
304	            height={PICKER_SIZE}
305	            className="block cursor-crosshair"
306	            style={{ width: PICKER_SIZE, height: PICKER_SIZE }}
307	            onMouseDown={handleMouseDown}
308	          />
309	          <div className="text-text-faint mt-2 flex justify-between text-sm">
310	            <span>Hue {hue}°</span>
311	            <span>Sat {Math.round(saturation * 100)}%</span>
312	          </div>
313	          <ColorInput
314	            currentHex={currentHex}
315	            targetInput={targetInput}
316	            targetFormat={targetFormat}
317	            hue={hue}
318	            saturation={saturation}
319	            onApply={setTarget}
320	          />
321	          <Popover.Arrow className="fill-surface-overlay" />
322	        </Popover.Content>
323	      </Popover.Portal>
324	    </Popover.Root>
325	  );
326	}
327
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx`

```text
1	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
2	import { formatAllFormats, type AllFormats } from "../../lib/colors";
3	import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
4	
5	const LABELS: Array<{ key: keyof AllFormats; label: string }> = [
6	  { key: "hex", label: "HEX" },
7	  { key: "rgb", label: "RGB" },
8	  { key: "hsl", label: "HSL" },
9	  { key: "oklch", label: "OKLCH" },
10	];
11	
12	export function TargetFormats() {
13	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
14	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
15	  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
16	  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
17	  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
18	  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
19	
20	  if (targetInput === null) return null;
21	
22	  const drifted = hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat;
23	  const formats = formatAllFormats(hue, saturation);
24	
25	  return (
26	    <div
27	      className={`border-border-default mt-3 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-x-4 gap-y-1 border-t pt-3 ${drifted ? "opacity-55" : ""}`}
28	      aria-label="Target color formats"
29	    >
30	      <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
31	        Target{drifted ? " (drifted)" : ""}
32	      </span>
33	      {LABELS.map(({ key, label }) => {
34	        const value = formats[key];
35	        const isCopied = copiedLabel === label;
36	        return (
37	          <button
38	            key={key}
39	            type="button"
40	            onClick={() => copy(value, label)}
41	            className="hover:bg-surface-hover group/fmt flex cursor-pointer items-baseline gap-1.5 px-1.5 py-0.5 text-left transition-colors"
42	            aria-label={`Copy ${label} ${value}`}
43	          >
44	            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
45	              {label}
46	            </span>
47	            <span className="text-text-secondary group-hover/fmt:text-text-primary font-mono text-xs tabular-nums">
48	              {isCopied ? "Copied" : value}
49	            </span>
50	          </button>
51	        );
52	      })}
53	    </div>
54	  );
55	}
56
```

```text
1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	
3	const WCAG_AA_CONTRAST = 4.5;
4	
5	export function getContrastColor(bgColor: string): string {
6	  const whiteContrast = chroma.contrast("white", bgColor);
7	  return whiteContrast < WCAG_AA_CONTRAST ? "#1d1d1d" : "#ffffff";
8	}
9	
10	export type ColorFormat = "hex" | "hsl" | "rgb" | "oklch" | "named";
11	
12	/**
13	 * Parse a color string and extract hue (0-360), saturation (0-1), and input format.
14	 * Supports hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), and named CSS colors.
15	 */
16	export function parseTargetColor(
17	  input: string,
18	): { hue: number; saturation: number; format: ColorFormat } | null {
19	  const trimmed = input.trim();
20	  if (!trimmed) return null;
21	
22	  try {
23	    // oklch() - chroma.js can't parse this natively
24	    const oklchMatch = trimmed.match(
25	      /oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i,
26	    );
27	    if (oklchMatch) {
28	      let l = parseFloat(oklchMatch[1]);
29	      if (oklchMatch[1].endsWith("%")) l /= 100;
30	      const c = parseFloat(oklchMatch[2]);
31	      const h = parseFloat(oklchMatch[3]);
32	      const [hue, sat] = chroma.oklch(l, c, h).hsl();
33	      return {
34	        hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
35	        saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
36	        format: "oklch",
37	      };
38	    }
39	
40	    // Detect format before parsing
41	    const lower = trimmed.toLowerCase();
42	    let format: ColorFormat = "named";
43	    if (lower.startsWith("#")) format = "hex";
44	    else if (lower.startsWith("hsl")) format = "hsl";
45	    else if (lower.startsWith("rgb")) format = "rgb";
46	
47	    const [hue, sat] = chroma(trimmed).hsl();
48	    return {
49	      hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
50	      saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
51	      format,
52	    };
53	  } catch {
54	    return null;
55	  }
56	}
57	
58	/**
59	 * Format a color at hue/sat/L=50 in the given format.
60	 */
61	export function formatTargetColor(
62	  hue: number,
63	  saturation: number,
64	  format: ColorFormat,
65	): string {
66	  const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5);
67	  switch (format) {
68	    case "hsl":
69	      return `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;
70	    case "rgb": {
71	      const [r, g, b] = color.rgb();
72	      return `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;
73	    }
74	    case "oklch":
75	      return toOklchString(color.hex());
76	    default:
77	      return color.hex();
78	  }
79	}
80	
81	export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
82	  const [l, c, h] = chroma(hex).oklch();
83	  const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
84	  const cR = Math.round((c || 0) * 1000) / 1000;
85	  const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
86	  return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
87	}
88	
89	export interface AllFormats {
90	  hex: string;
91	  rgb: string;
92	  hsl: string;
93	  oklch: string;
94	}
95	
96	/**
97	 * Return all four string representations of a color at hue/sat/L=50.
98	 */
99	export function formatAllFormats(hue: number, saturation: number): AllFormats {
100	  return {
101	    hex: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hex"),
102	    rgb: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "rgb"),
103	    hsl: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hsl"),
104	    oklch: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "oklch"),
105	  };
106	}
107
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
{"success":true,"message":"Message sent to team-lead's inbox","routing":{"sender":"feature-reviewer","senderColor":"green","target":"@team-lead","summary":"PR #5 target color feature review findings","content":"PR #5 target color feature review complete. Reviewed: `TargetSwatch.tsx` (326 lines, new), `TargetFormats.tsx` (55 lines, new), `lib/colors.ts` (90 lines added), `paletteStore.ts` (+32 lines), `types/palette.ts` (formatting only).\n\n---\n\n## Critical\n\nNone.\n\n---\n\n## High\n\n**`lib/colors.ts:71` — `color.rgb()` returns floats; output is invalid CSS**\n`formatTargetColor` for `\"rgb\"` format does:\n```ts\nconst [r, g, b] = color.rgb();\nreturn `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;\n```\nchroma.js `.rgb()` returns raw floats. For non-pure-hue colors (e.g. `hsl(30, 50%, 50%)`), this produces `rgb(191.25,143.75000000000003,63.75)` — invalid CSS. Browsers will reject or misinterpret it, and copy/paste to any tool will fail silently.\n**Fix:** `return \\`rgb(${Math.round(r)},${Math.round(g)},${Math.round(b)})\\`;`\nConfidence: 90\n\n---\n\n## Important\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx:80–92` — Text input has no accessible label**\nThe `<input>` inside `ColorInput` has no `aria-label` or associated `<label>`. Only a `placeholder` attribute. Placeholder disappears on type and screen readers don't treat it as a label. Keyboard/AT users won't know what the field is for.\n**Fix:** Add `aria-label=\"Enter target color (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch)\"` to the input.\nConfidence: 85\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx:286–294` — Clear button invisible to keyboard focus**\nThe clear-target button uses `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100` with no `focus-visible:opacity-100`. It's in the DOM and focusable, but fully invisible when tabbed to — a WCAG 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance) failure.\n**Fix:** Add `focus-visible:opacity-100` to the button's class list.\nConfidence: 85\n\n**`TargetFormats.tsx:27` — `opacity-55` container opacity violates token guidelines**\nCLAUDE.md: \"Use design tokens instead of hardcoded HSL or opacity tricks.\" The \"drifted\" state is implemented with `opacity-55` on the whole container, which compounds with existing text token opacities and may push `text-text-faint` below WCAG AA contrast. Should use lower-tier tokens on individual elements (e.g. swap `text-text-secondary` → `text-text-faint`) rather than blanket opacity.\nConfidence: 80\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx:231–244` — Canvas picker has no touch/pointer support**\nMouse events only (`mousemove`, `mouseup` on document, `onMouseDown` on canvas). Touch users and stylus users cannot drag the color picker. The rest of the UI (sliders via Radix) is touch-accessible, so this is an inconsistency.\n**Fix:** Replace with pointer events (`onPointerDown`, `pointermove`, `pointerup`) which unify mouse + touch + stylus.\nConfidence: 80\n\n---\n\n## Nit (below threshold, flagging anyway)\n\n**`lib/colors.ts:69` — HSL output lacks spaces after commas**\n`hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)` is technically valid CSS but the canonical format uses spaces. More visibly: if a user types `hsl(120, 75%, 50%)` (with spaces), the copy-feedback line shows `hsl(120,75%,50%)` (without), creating a jarring inconsistency.\nConfidence: 70 (not reported as a finding, just calling it out)\n\n---\n\n## State Management\n\nNo issues. `setTarget` correctly updates both live hue/saturation and target snapshot in a single `set()` call — no partial-state race. `clearTarget` is symmetric. No stale closures visible.\n\n## Color Parsing\n\n`parseTargetColor` logic is sound: oklch is pre-parsed (chroma.js can't handle it), format detection is correct for hex/hsl/rgb/named, NaN guards on achromatic colors are handled. The \"named\" format fallback to hex in `formatTargetColor` is intentional and correct.\n\n---\n\nTask #1 marked complete."}}
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-17 06:45:29Z)**_

Review complete. Here's a summary of what I found across the target color feature:

**1 High finding:**
- `lib/colors.ts:71` — `color.rgb()` returns raw floats from chroma.js. The RGB format output will produce invalid CSS like `rgb(191.25,143.75000000000003,63.75)` for non-pure-hue colors. Fix: wrap each channel with `Math.round()`.

**4 Important findings:**
- `TargetSwatch.tsx:80–92` — Color text input has no `aria-label` (placeholder ≠ label for screen readers)
- `TargetSwatch.tsx:286–294` — Clear button is keyboard-focusable but invisible when focused (missing `focus-visible:opacity-100`)
- `TargetFormats.tsx:27` — `opacity-55` on the drifted container violates the project's token guidelines and can push already-dim text below WCAG AA contrast
- `TargetSwatch.tsx:231–244` — Canvas picker is mouse-only; touch/stylus users can't drag it (fix: use pointer events)

State management and color parsing logic are clean — no issues there.

---
